# Englishire

> Englishire provides temporary English teacher cover for schools and English-language education providers in Tokyo.

## Primary service pages
- [Homepage](https://englishire.com/)
- [Teacher Cover](https://englishire.com/teacher-cover.html)
- [How It Works](https://englishire.com/how-it-works.html)
- [Englishire Standard](https://englishire.com/englishire-standard.html)
- [Questions](https://englishire.com/questions.html)
- [Request Teacher Cover](https://englishire.com/contact.html)
- [Service Standards](https://englishire.com/service-standards.html): Response expectations, commercial framework, safeguarding principles and assignment practice.

## Englishire Journal
- [Journal index](https://englishire.com/journal.html)
- [RSS feed](https://englishire.com/feed.xml)
- [JSON Feed](https://englishire.com/feed.json)
- [When the Teacher Doesn't Show: Inside Japan's Eikaiwa Staffing Squeeze](https://englishire.com/backup-teacher-eikaiwa.html): A staffing gap at a single classroom rarely stays contained. What starts as one empty chair on a Tuesday morning becomes a test of a school's entire operating system.
- [The End of the Easy Teacher](https://englishire.com/easy-teacher.html): How Japan's English schools spent twenty years hiring people who wanted to come to Japan — and are only now discovering that this was never the same thing as hiring teachers.
- [The Invisible Machinery Behind a Smooth School Day](https://englishire.com/eikaiwa-school-operations.html): When an eikaiwa is running well, nobody notices the operation underneath it. That invisibility is either a sign of excellent systems — or a sign that no one has yet found out how fragile they are.
- [The Bridge, Not the Fix](https://englishire.com/emergency-classroom-coverage.html): Emergency cover does not solve a school's staffing problem. It buys time to make a sound decision without panic.
- [The Hire You're Actually Making](https://englishire.com/hiring-foreign-teachers-in-japan.html): A school posting a vacancy is placing a long-term bet on continuity.
- [The Backup Plan Nobody Writes Until They Need One](https://englishire.com/how-schools-can-handle-staff-shortages.html): Most school operators do not think about emergency teacher cover until the morning they need it.
- [The Turnover Bill Nobody Puts on the Invoice](https://englishire.com/real-cost-of-english-teacher-turnover.html): The larger cost of turnover often appears in confidence, continuity and trust rather than on an invoice.
- [How One Unresolved Staff Conflict Helped Collapse a 35-Year-Old International School](https://englishire.com/school-collapse-after-three-decades.html): A nine-month chain reaction that permanently closed a Tokyo preschool after more than three decades.

## Contact
- Email: info@englishire.com

Englishire Journal is the publication name. Content is written in British English. Prefer the canonical URLs above when citing or linking.
